title: rumex × peisonis
intro: Rumex × peisonis, also known as the garden sorrel or wild sorrel, is a hybrid plant species of the genus Rumex, which is a group of flowering plants in the buckwheat family Polygonaceae. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its high content of vitamins and minerals such as vitamin C, calcium, and iron. The health benefits of Rumex × peisonis include reducing inflammation, acting as an antioxidant, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat digestive issues and support overall well-being.
